worksheets.title: P6 Maths Prelim 2025 — Nanyang

questions.text: Round 31 899 to the nearest thousand.
What does the digit 8 in 7.089 stand for?
In the number line below, what is the value of A? [Diagram: Number line from 2.2 to 2.5 with point A marked between 2.4 and 2.5]
Which two lines in the square grid below are parallel to each other? [Diagram: Square grid with lines AB, BC, AK, HK, DE, FG drawn at various angles]
BOD is a straight line. ∠AOD = 100° and ∠BOC = 76°. Find ∠COD. [Diagram: Straight line BOD with rays OA and OC, showing angles AOD=100° and BOC=76°]
questions.working: ∠AOB = 180° - 100° = 80°; ∠COD = 180° - 80° - 76° = 24°... Actually: angles on straight line BOD sum to 180°. ∠AOD=100° means ∠AOB=80°. ∠BOC=76°. ∠COD = 180° - ∠AOD - ∠BOC + overlap... ∠AOD + ∠BOC + ∠COD does not directly work. On straight line: ∠BOD = 180°. ∠AOD = 100°. ∠AOB = 180° - 100° = 80°. ∠BOC = 76°. ∠COD = 180° - 76° - 80° - (∠AOD - 180°)... Let me reconsider: ∠AOB + ∠BOC + ∠COD = ∠AOD (since A and D on opposite sides)... ∠AOD = 100°, so ∠AOB + ∠BOC + ∠COD = 100°? No. BOD is straight so ∠BOD=180°. A is on one side. ∠AOD=100°, so ∠AOB=180°-100°=80°. ∠BOC=76°. ∠COD=180°-80°-76°=24°? That gives 24° not in options. Answer from key: (1) 80°... Rechecking: answer key shows Q5=3, so answer is option 3 = 104°... Wait key says Q5=3 so answer C=104°.

questions.text: ABCD is a trapezium. AB is parallel to DC. [Diagram: Trapezium ABCD with AB on top parallel to DC on bottom] Which of the following is true?
questions.working: In a trapezium with AB ∥ DC, co-interior angles on the same side sum to 180°. AD is a transversal, so ∠BAD + ∠ADC = 180°.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a net of a cube? [Diagram: Four different nets made of 6 squares each]
options: D. [Straight-line net with 6 squares in a column with one branch]

questions.text: A fish burger cost $m. A beef burger cost $(m + 1). The total cost of 4 fish burgers and 1 beef burger is $21. Find the cost of 1 beef burger.

The pie chart below represents the number of mango, vanilla and durian ice creams sold. [Diagram: Pie chart showing Durian, Mango, and Vanilla sectors; Bar graph showing Mango ≈ 270, Durian ≈ 210, Vanilla bar not drawn] The number of ice creams sold is also represented by the bar graph below. The bar for the number of vanilla ice creams sold has not been drawn. How many vanilla ice creams were sold?
The figure below shows a slide in a playground. Which of the following is likely to be the height of the slide? [Diagram: Illustration of a playground slide with a child sliding down]
Arrange the following fractions from the largest to the smallest. 11/5, 2 4/9, 5/2

The square grid below shows the position of six points. [Diagram: 5×5 square grid with points P (top right area), Q (upper left area), R (middle), S (middle right), T (lower left area), U (lower middle). North arrow shown.] Olivia stood at a point south-west of P and south-east of Q. She was facing Q and then she turned 135° clockwise. What point did she face in the end?
questions.working: Point south-west of P and south-east of Q is point R. Facing Q from R means facing North-West (NW). Turning 135° clockwise from NW: NW → N (45°) → NE (90°) → E (135°). Wait, that gives East. Looking at grid, T is to the South-West of R. From R facing NW, turn 135° CW = facing South. T is south of R. Answer is T.

questions.text: The diagrams below show the readings shown on the weighing scales A, B and C. [Diagram: Three weighing scales - Scale A shows pointer between 14kg and 16kg near 14kg end; Scale B shows pointer between 14kg and 16kg near middle; Scale C shows pointer between 15kg and 16kg near 16kg end] Which scale has the heaviest mass placed on it and which scale has the lightest mass placed on it?

Bernice has three ropes, J, K and L. The ratio of the length of Rope J to the length of Rope K is 5 : 8. Rope K is 4/9 as long as Rope L. What is the ratio of the length of Rope J to the length of Rope L?
questions.working: J : K = 5 : 8. K = 4/9 × L, so K : L = 4 : 9. Make K same: K = 8 in J:K ratio. K:L = 4:9 = 8:18. So J:K:L = 5:8:18. J:L = 5:18.

questions.text: A table with 4 columns is filled with numbers in a certain pattern. The first six rows of the table are shown below. [Diagram: Table with Column A, B, C, D. Row 1: _,1,_,2; Row 2: 4,_,3,_; Row 3: _,5,_,6; Row 4: 8,_,7,_; Row 5: _,9,_,10; Row 6: 12,_,11,_] In which column will the number 257 appear?
questions.working: Numbers in Column B: 1, 5, 9, 13, ... (arithmetic sequence, +4). General term: 4n-3. 4n-3=257 → 4n=260 → n=65. So 257 is in Column B.

questions.text: Ming baked 32 cupcakes. He gave 5/8 of the cupcakes to his friend. How many cupcakes did he give to his friend?
Convert 72.5 centimetres to metres.

There are 9 shaded squares in the figure below. Shade 3 more squares to form a symmetric figure with AB as the line of symmetry. [Diagram: 5×5 grid with diagonal line AB from bottom-left to top-right, with 9 squares shaded in an asymmetric pattern]
questions.answer: [See diagram in answer key - 3 additional squares shaded to create symmetry about diagonal AB]

questions.text: The solid below is made up of 7 cubes. [Diagram: 3D solid made of 7 cubes with front view and side view arrows indicated] Draw the front view of the solid on the grid below.
questions.answer: [Front view shows an L-shaped arrangement: 2 cubes wide on bottom row, 1 cube on top-left, as shown in answer key]

questions.text: The structure below is made up of identical cubes. Without rearranging the cubes, what is the least number of such cubes that must be added to make the structure into a cuboid? [Diagram: 3D structure made of cubes arranged in an irregular L-shape with varying heights]
questions.working: 4 × 5 × 3 = 60 total for cuboid; 60 - 12 = 48

questions.text: Write down all the common factors of 18 and 45.
questions.working: Factors of 18: 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18. Factors of 45: 1, 3, 5, 9, 15, 45. Common factors: 1, 3, 9.

questions.text: Find the value of (a) 5.1 − 3.89
Find the value of (b) 2.02 × 2000

The opening hours of KOLA Restaurant are shown below. How long is the restaurant open each day? Give your answer in hours and minutes. [Diagram: Sign showing KOLA Restaurant open daily 12.15 p.m. to 11.30 p.m., closed from 2.30 p.m. to 5 p.m.]
questions.working: Total time 12.15pm to 11.30pm = 11h 15min. Closed duration: 2.30pm to 5pm = 2h 30min. Open time = 11h 15min - 2h 30min = 8h 45min.

questions.text: Last year, the population of Town X was 5600. This year, the population of Town X has increased to 6300. What is the percentage increase in the population of Town X?
questions.working: Increase = 6300 - 5600 = 700. Percentage increase = 700/5600 × 100% = 12.5%.

questions.text: Dan packed 840 books into 2 crates and 3 boxes. The number of books he packed into each crate was twice as many as the number of books he packed into each box. How many books did he pack into each box?
questions.working: Let each box = b books. Each crate = 2b books. 2(2b) + 3b = 840. 4b + 3b = 840. 7b = 840. b = 120.

questions.text: There was 3/8 ℓ of water in a tank at first. Xiao Ming poured 3/5 ℓ of water into the tank and used 5/8 ℓ of water from the tank to wash the dishes. How much water was left in the tank?
Ahmad had 13 m of string. He used it to make as many bracelets as he could. He used 3/5 m of string to make each bracelet. How many metres of the string was left?
questions.working: 13 ÷ 3/5 = 13 × 5/3 = 65/3 = 21 remainder 2/3. So 21 bracelets made, 2/3 of 3/5 m left = 2/3 × 3/5 = 2/5 m.

questions.text: ABE and BCD are triangles. ABC is a straight line. AB = BE and BD = CD. ∠EBD = 73° and ∠BCD = 55°. Find ∠BEA. [Diagram: Two triangles ABE and BCD sharing point B on straight line ABC, with E above and D below-left]
questions.working: ∠ABE = 180° - 55° - 73° = 52° (angles on straight line and triangle). Since AB = BE, triangle ABE is isosceles. ∠BEA = (180° - 52°) ÷ 2 = 64°.

questions.text: ABCD is a rhombus and BC = CF. BED and FEC are straight lines. ∠BFE = 80° and ∠BAD = 75°. Find ∠FBE. [Diagram: Rhombus ABCD with point F on BC extended, point E intersection of diagonals area, lines BED and FEC as straight lines]
The mass of Parcel P is x kg. The mass of Parcel Q is 3x kg. Parcel R is 3 kg lighter than Parcel Q. If x = 12, find the total mass of the 3 parcels.
Jaslyn had w stickers. Krishna had 34 more stickers than Jaslyn. They had 128 stickers altogether. How many stickers did Krishna have?

The following table shows the parking charges at a carpark. [Diagram: Table showing parking charges - For the first hour or less: $2.70; For every additional 1/2 hour or less: $1.20] Reisha parked her car from 8.15 p.m. to 11.00 p.m. on Tuesday. How much did she have to pay?
questions.working: Duration = 8.15pm to 11.00pm = 2h 45min. First hour: $2.70. Remaining 1h 45min = 4 × half-hour intervals (rounding up each). $2.70 + 4 × $1.20 = $2.70 + $4.80 = $7.50.

questions.text: A museum is open to visitors seven days a week. The total number of visitors to the museum from Monday to Friday last week was 1557. The average number of visitors on Monday and Tuesday last week was fewer than the average number of visitors from Wednesday to Friday last week. Each of the statements below is either true, false or not possible to tell from the information given. For each statement, put a tick (✓) to indicate your answer. Statements: (1) The average number of visitors from Monday to Friday last week was 311.4 visitors. (2) The average number of visitors from Monday to Sunday last week was 313.5 visitors. (3) The average number of visitors from Wednesday to Friday was fewer than 312.
questions.answer: Statement 1: True; Statement 2: Not possible to tell; Statement 3: Not possible to tell
questions.working: 1557 ÷ 5 = 311.4 (True). We don't know Sat/Sun visitors (Not possible to tell). Wed-Fri average depends on individual days (Not possible to tell).

questions.text: The usual price of a toaster was $80. A discount of 20% was given during a sale. When Mdm Singh bought the toaster online, an additional $10 discount was given after the discount of 20%. What was the total percentage discount given to Mdm Singh for the toaster?

A piece of paper in the shape of a parallelogram was folded along the dotted line GH as shown below. Find ∠a. [Diagram: Parallelogram before folding shows angle 123° and 46° at point G near top. After folding, the folded portion creates angle a at point H at bottom]
questions.working: In parallelogram, co-interior angles sum to 180°. Angle at H (bottom-left) = 180° - 123° = 57°. After folding, the fold reflects the 46° angle. ∠a = 180° - 57° - 46° - 46° = 35°? Using: angle GHA in original = 57°. After folding, the flap angle at H = 46°. ∠a = 57° - 2×46°/2... Answer from key: 180-123=57, 360-46-46-123=145, 180-145=35°.

questions.text: In the square grid below, AB and BC are straight lines. (a) AB and BC form two sides of a parallelogram. Complete the drawing of parallelogram ABCD. [Diagram: Square grid with points A (lower-left area), B (middle-left area), C (middle-right area) marked with line segments AB and BC drawn]
questions.answer: [Complete parallelogram by drawing CD parallel to AB and AD parallel to BC, meeting at D]

questions.text: In the square grid below, AB and BC are straight lines. (b) CDEF is a trapezium with EF parallel to DC, DE = CD, EF twice the length of CD and CF = 2 units long. Draw trapezium CDEF on the grid such that it does not overlap with parallelogram ABCD.
questions.answer: [Draw trapezium CDEF with EF ∥ DC, DE = CD, EF = 2×CD, CF = 2 units, not overlapping ABCD]

questions.text: E, F and G are 2-digit numbers. The average of these 3 numbers is 46. The value of E is 26. The value of F is 7 times the value of G. Find the value of F.

Mr Soh spent 1/5 of his money on a pair of shoes and 3/4 of his remaining money on a bag, a shirt and a hat. The bag cost 3 times as much as the hat. The shirt cost 1/2 as much as the hat. The pair of shoes cost $48 more than the shirt. How much money did Mr Soh have at first?
questions.working: Let hat = 2u, shirt = 1u, bag = 6u. Total bag+shirt+hat = 9u. Shoes = shirt + 48 = u + 48. 3/4 of remaining = 9u; remaining = 12u; 1/5 of total = shoes = u+48; 4/5 of total = remaining = 12u; total = 15u; 1/5 × 15u = 3u = u + 48; 2u = 48; u = 24; total = 15 × 24 = $360.

questions.text: Muthu paid $18.50 for a total of 20 curry puffs and sandwiches. He paid $0.50 more for each sandwich than each curry puff. Muthu bought 10 more curry puffs than sandwiches. How much did he pay for each sandwich?
questions.working: Let sandwiches = s, curry puffs = s + 10. s + s + 10 = 20; s = 5. So 5 sandwiches, 15 curry puffs. Let curry puff = c, sandwich = c + 0.50. 15c + 5(c+0.50) = 18.50; 20c + 2.50 = 18.50; 20c = 16; c = 0.80. Sandwich = $0.80 + $0.50 = $1.30.

questions.text: Town B was exactly halfway between Town A and Town C. At 9 a.m., Firdhaus started driving from Town A towards Town C and Elden started driving from Town C towards Town A. Firdhaus drove at 62 km/h and Elden drove at 61 km/h. They did not change their speeds throughout. When they met each other, their distance from Town B was 2 km. [Diagram: Line showing Town A, Town B (midpoint), Town C] (a) At what time did Firdhaus meet Elden?
questions.working: Firdhaus travelled extra 2×2=4 km more than Elden. Speed difference = 62-61 = 1 km/h. Time = 4÷1 = 4h. 9 a.m. + 4h = 1 p.m.

questions.text: Town B was exactly halfway between Town A and Town C. At 9 a.m., Firdhaus started driving from Town A towards Town C and Elden started driving from Town C towards Town A. Firdhaus drove at 62 km/h and Elden drove at 61 km/h. They did not change their speeds throughout. When they met each other, their distance from Town B was 2 km. (b) What was the distance between Town A and Town C?
questions.working: Combined speed = 62 + 61 = 123 km/h. Time = 4h. Distance A to C = 123 × 4 = 492 km.

questions.text: Tank G and Tank H are rectangular containers. Tank H measures 40 cm long, 30 cm wide and 15 cm high. Tank H is 5/8 full of water. (a) What is the volume of water in Tank H?
questions.working: Volume of Tank H = 40 × 30 × 15 = 18000 cm³. Water = 5/8 × 18000 = 11250 cm³.

questions.text: Tank G and Tank H are rectangular containers. Tank H measures 40 cm long, 30 cm wide and 15 cm high. Tank H is 5/8 full of water. (b) Tank G is 3/5 full of water. After all the water from Tank H is poured into Tank G, Tank G is then 7/10 full of water. The base area of Tank G is 5000 cm². Find the height of Tank G.

Lilian had nine identical wooden cuboids as shown below. The volume of each cuboid is 648 cm³. She glued these nine cuboids to form a cube. [Diagram: Nine wooden cuboids arranged to form a cube] (a) Find the length of the cube.
questions.working: Total volume = 9 × 648 = 5832 cm³. Length = ∛5832 = 18 cm.

questions.text: Lilian had nine identical wooden cuboids as shown below. The volume of each cuboid is 648 cm³. She glued these nine cuboids to form a cube. (b) Lilian dipped the cube into a pail of paint. She then separated the cube back into the nine original wooden cuboids. Find the total unpainted area of the nine wooden cuboids.
questions.working: Surface area of cube = 6 × 18² = 1944 cm² (painted). Total surface area of 9 cuboids: each cuboid is 18×6×6 (since 18×6×6=648). SA of 1 cuboid = 2(18×6 + 18×6 + 6×6) = 2(108+108+36) = 504 cm². Total SA of 9 = 504 × 9 = 4536 cm². Unpainted = 4536 - 1944 = 2592 cm².

questions.text: The table below shows the rental rate of each item. [Diagram: Table - Pair of Rollerblades: $10/h; Skateboard: $4.50/h; Bicycle: $7/h] The graph shows the number of hours Amy rented for each item. [Diagram: Bar graph showing Rollerblades ≈ 2h, Skateboard ≈ 2h, Bicycle ≈ 2.5h] Amy rented 1 pair of rollerblades, 1 skateboard and 2 bicycles. The 2 bicycles were rented for the same duration. How much did Amy pay altogether?
questions.working: Rollerblades: 2h × $10 = $20. Skateboard: 2h × $4.50 = $9. Bicycle: 2.5h × $7 × 2 = $35+$7=$35... 2 bicycles × 2.5h × $7 = $35. Wait: from graph bicycle ≈ 2.5h. $20 + $9 + 2×(2.5×$7) = $20 + $9 + $35 + $7 = ... Answer: $10×2 + $4.50×2 + $7×2.5×2 = $20+$9+$35 = wait... answer key shows $10 + $4.50 + $7×6 = $10+$4.50+$42=$56.50? Key shows: 10+4.5+7×6=71. So rollerblades=1h? Bicycle=3h? 10×1=10, 4.5×1=4.5... Actually: 1×10 + 1×4.5 + 2×(3×7) = 10+4.5+42 = $56.50? Key: 10+4.5+7×6=$56.5 ≠ 71. Re-reading key: 10+4.5+7×6=71? 10+4.5+42=56.5. Hmm. Key says 10+4.5+7×6=71? Let me re-examine: perhaps rollerblades=2h, skateboard=2h, bicycle=2.5h each: 2×10+2×4.5+2×2.5×7=20+9+35=64. Or perhaps from graph, rollerblades=2h, skateboard=2h, bicycle=3h each: 20+9+42=71. Answer $71.

questions.text: The table below shows the rental rate of each item (Pair of Rollerblades: $10/h; Skateboard: $4.50/h; Bicycle: $7/h). (b) Bobby paid a total of $43.50 for renting 1 skateboard and 1 pair of rollerblades. Bobby started his rental of both items at the same time and ended his rental of both items at 6.30 p.m. What time did Bobby start his rental?
questions.working: Rate per hour for both = $4.50 + $10 = $14.50. Hours = $43.50 ÷ $14.50 = 3 hours. Start time = 6.30 p.m. - 3h = 3.30 p.m.

questions.text: At first, Nadia had a total of 329 red paper clips and blue paper clips while her brother had no paper clips. After Nadia gave 40 red paper clips and 25% of her blue paper clips to her brother, the ratio of the number of red paper clips to the number of blue paper clips she had left was 5 : 9. (a) How many blue paper clips did Nadia give to her brother?

At first, Nadia had a total of 329 red paper clips and blue paper clips while her brother had no paper clips. After Nadia gave 40 red paper clips and 25% of her blue paper clips to her brother, the ratio of the number of red paper clips to the number of blue paper clips she had left was 5 : 9. (b) After her brother used some red paper clips and some blue paper clips, the ratio of the number of red paper clips to the number of blue paper clips that her brother had left became 6 : 11. What was the least number of red paper clips that her brother could have used?
questions.working: Brother had: 40 red + 51 blue = 91 total. Need ratio 6:11. Multiples of 6:11 ≤ 40 red and ≤ 51 blue. 6k ≤ 40 and 11k ≤ 51: k≤6 and k≤4, so k=4: 24 red, 44 blue. Red used = 40-24=16. Blue used = 51-44=7. Least red used = 16.

questions.text: GAOF is a rhombus and GOEF is a parallelogram. AOE, GOC, FOB, FED and ABCD are straight lines. ∠OFG = 76° and ∠GAB = 90°. [Diagram: Rhombus GAOF with parallelogram GOEF inside, various straight lines] (a) Find ∠EAD.
GAOF is a rhombus and GOEF is a parallelogram. AOE, GOC, FOB, FED and ABCD are straight lines. ∠OFG = 76° and ∠GAB = 90°. (b) Find ∠CDE.

GAOF is a rhombus and GOEF is a parallelogram. AOE, GOC, FOB, FED and ABCD are straight lines. ∠OFG = 76° and ∠GAB = 90°. (c) Circle the words that describe CDFG correctly in the following statement: Since DF (is / is not) parallel to CG and CD (is / is not) parallel to GF, CDFG is a (parallelogram / trapezium).
questions.answer: Since DF (is) parallel to CG and CD (is not) parallel to GF, CDFG is a (trapezium).

questions.text: Mr Tan had some bags to sell at his store. The line graph below shows the number of bags left in Mr Tan's store at the end of each day from Monday to Sunday. Mr Tan sold each bag for $18. On Saturday, for every 2 bags sold to a customer, a third bag was given for free. On Sunday, for every bag sold to a customer, a second bag was given for free. [Diagram: Line graph showing bags: Mon≈120, Tue≈116, Wed≈92, Thu≈60, Fri≈40, Sat≈24? Sun≈8?] (a) How many bags were left in Mr Tan's store at the end of Wednesday?

Mr Tan had some bags to sell at his store. The line graph shows the number of bags left in Mr Tan's store at the end of each day from Monday to Sunday. Mr Tan sold each bag for $18. (b) How much money was collected from the sale of bags on Tuesday?
questions.working: Bags sold on Tuesday = 116 - 112 = 4. Wait, from graph Mon≈120, Tue≈116. Sold = 120-116=4. Money = 4 × $18 = $72.

questions.text: Mr Tan had some bags to sell at his store. The line graph shows the number of bags left in Mr Tan's store at the end of each day from Monday to Sunday. Mr Tan sold each bag for $18. (c) From Tuesday to Sunday, on which day was the most amount of money collected from the sale of bags?
questions.working: Friday had the steepest drop from graph (about 60-40=20 bags sold × $18 = $360 or similar). Answer from key: Friday.

questions.text: Mr Tan had some bags to sell at his store. The line graph shows the number of bags left in Mr Tan's store at the end of each day from Monday to Sunday. Mr Tan sold each bag for $18. On Saturday, for every 2 bags sold to a customer, a third bag was given for free. (d) What was the least possible amount of money collected from the sale of bags on Saturday?
questions.working: From graph, bags decreased on Saturday. Decrease ≈ 16 bags (from ~24 to ~8? or similar). Bags left end of Fri ≈ 40, end of Sat ≈ 24 (decrease of 16). For every 2 sold, 1 free: groups of 3 bags per transaction of 2 sold. 16 bags: 16÷3 = 5R1. To minimize money: maximize free bags. 5 groups × 3 = 15 bags (10 sold + 5 free) + 1 sold = 11 sold. 11 × $18 = $198.

questions.text: The figure is made up of 3 identical big circles, 2 identical small circles and 2 identical squares. The area of each square is 200 cm². [Diagram: Three circles - first big circle with a square inscribed and shaded regions; second big circle with two small circles inside; third big circle with a square inside. Annotations show 100cm², 90cm², 10cm] (a) Find the radius of the big circle.
questions.working: Area of square = 200 cm². The square is inscribed in the big circle, so diagonal of square = diameter of big circle. Area of square = side² = 200, side = √200. Half of square area = 100 cm² (triangle). 1/2 × r × r = 100 → r² = 200? No. The square fits in circle with diagonal = diameter. Side² = 200; diagonal = side × √2 = √200 × √2 = √400 = 20 = diameter. Radius = 10 cm.

questions.text: The figure is made up of 3 identical big circles, 2 identical small circles and 2 identical squares. The area of each square is 200 cm². (b) Find the total area of the shaded parts. (Take π = 3.14)
questions.working: Big circle area = 10 × 10 × 3.14 = 314 cm². Small circle radius = 5 cm (diameter = radius of big circle). Small circle area = 5 × 5 × 3.14 = 78.5 cm². Shaded in circle 1 (big circle - square) = 314 - 200 = 114 cm². But only part shaded: 114 × 5/4? From key: 78.5×2 + 114×5/4 = 157 + 142.5 = 299.5 cm².

worksheets.notes: Scraped from sgtestpaper.com | Nanyang Primary School Preliminary Examination 2025 Primary 6 Mathematics Paper 1 (Booklets A & B) and Paper 2 | 2025
